description: §48.3.3 / §48.5.3 clean neg — a READ-ONLY use of a reactive `@`-cell (`return a + @value`) inside a pure `fn` body SHALL NOT fire E-FN-003. Pure-value reads of `@`-variables are permitted (E-FN-009, the live-subscription capture case, is deferred); only WRITES are the mutation the code targets. `notCodes` asserts E-FN-003 stays silent.
rationale: §48.3.3 targets outer-scope MUTATION; reading an `@`-cell as a pure value (§48.5.3) is not a mutation and is allowed inside `fn`. E-FN-003 fires on `@x = ...`, not on `... + @x`.
